findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 44. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements create invalid HTML structure. The document has a <main> element wrapping the entire page content, and then another <main> element inside it that contains the actual document body.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <body><main><header>...</header><main>...</main></main></body> with two nested <main> elements.

findings.description: The table of contents in the HTML contains internal anchor links that do not exist in the PDF. These are navigational aids added by the converter rather than fabricated external links.
findings.evidence: HTML TOC has <a href="#history-background">, <a href="#march-1-2016-public-meeting-and-oral-comments-summary">, etc. The PDF TOC has no hyperlinks.

findings.description: The footer text from page 1 is reproduced as a visible element in the HTML body, appearing both in the header section and as an image placeholder area. The page headers ('Meeting Officer's Report / Coal Ash Impoundment Classification(s) / Sutton Energy Complex, New Hanover County / April 28, 2016 / Page X of 5') from pages 2-5 are correctly omitted.
findings.evidence: The footer block 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management / 1646 Mail Service Center...' appears in the header area of the HTML.

findings.description: Third image has empty alt text, which is uninformative. It appears to correspond to a decorative line or page separator in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML:
